steps.title: Parallel lines have equal slopes
steps.content: Line $k$ is $y = 6x + 4$. The slope is the coefficient of $x$: slope $= 6$. Line $j$ is parallel to $k$, so it has the same slope $= 6$.

steps.title: Use the given point to find the intercept
steps.content: Line $j$ passes through $(0, 5)$. A point of the form $(0, b)$ is the $y$-intercept directly, so $b = 5$. The equation of $j$ is $y = 6x + 5$. That matches choice A — A is correct.
